across.clue: The basic unit of all living things
across.hint: This is the smallest living unit; every organism is made of it

across.clue: Cell organisation level between tissue and organ system
across.hint: Your heart and lungs are examples of this level of organisation

across.clue: The jelly-like substance filling a cell
across.hint: Think of it as the 'filling' inside the cell membrane

across.clue: A type of microorganism that includes mushrooms and moulds
across.hint: This kingdom includes yeast, moulds and mushrooms

across.clue: Microbes that help convert atmospheric nitrogen into usable form
across.hint: This helpful bacterium lives in the root nodules of legume plants

down.clue: A plant cell has this rigid outer layer that animal cells lack
down.hint: Plants need this for structural support; animals do not have it

down.clue: An instrument used to observe objects invisible to the naked eye
down.hint: Anton van Leeuwenhoek used this to first observe microbes

down.clue: Organisms made of a single cell (e.g. Amoeba)
down.hint: Amoeba and Paramecium live their whole life as just ONE of these

down.clue: Microorganism used to make curd and yoghurt
down.hint: Leave milk warm overnight with this microbe — you get curd!

down.clue: The process by which microbes break down dead matter
down.hint: Without this, dead leaves would never disappear from the forest floor
